content.problemStatement:
Chef calls a sequence good if it does not contain any two adjacent identical elements.

Initially, Chef has a sequence a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äã. He likes to change the sequence every now and then. You should process QQQ queries. In each query, Chef chooses an index xxx and changes the xxx-th element of the sequence to yyy. After each query, can you find the length of the longest good **subsequence** of the current sequence?

Note that the queries are not independent.
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and QQQ.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers a1,a2,‚Ä¶,aNa_1, a_2, \ldots, a_Na1‚Äã,a2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,aN‚Äã.
QQQ lines follow. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers xxx and yyy describing a query.
content.outputFormat: After each query, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äï the length of the longest good subsequence.
